title: China-to-Ethiopia EV Charger Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: Ethiopia EVCS Directive No. 1034/2024 — Charging Infrastructure Mandate and IEC 61851 Modes
rows.target_requirement: The Ethiopian Petroleum and Energy Authority issued EVCS Directive No. 1034/2024 in December 2024, establishing a comprehensive legal framework for electric vehicle charging infrastructure in Ethiopia. The directive requires EV importers and assemblers to establish charging stations as a mandatory market-entry condition. Fast-charging stations must be available every 50 km (approximately 31 miles), and heavy-vehicle charging points every 120 km (approximately 75 miles). The directive references IEC 61851 for EV charging modes, specifying that charging shall comply with the four conductive charging modes defined in IEC 61851. Importers of EV chargers must provide internationally recognised battery safety and charger safety certifications. All EV chargers deployed in Ethiopia should be configured for the Ethiopian grid: 220 V single-phase or 380 V three-phase, 50 Hz.
target_standards: EVCS Directive No. 1034/2024 — Directive Issued to Determine Electric Vehicle Charging System (Ethiopian Petroleum and Energy Authority)
IEC 61851 — Electric vehicle conductive charging system (four charging modes referenced in the Directive)
Ethiopian Energy Proclamation 810/2013 and Energy Operations Regulation 447/2018 (EEU grid framework)
rows.china_equivalent: China's EV charger safety and charging-mode standard is GB/T 18487, which corresponds broadly to IEC 61851 but differs in connector interface, communication protocol (GB/T 27930 CAN-based vs IEC 61851 pilot signal), and some safety parameters. A charger built and certified solely to GB/T 18487 cannot be assumed to satisfy IEC 61851 mode requirements without technical review and, where needed, re-testing or adaptation.
china_standards: GB/T 18487.1-2023 — Electric vehicle conductive charging system — Part 1: General requirements (implemented April 1, 2024)
GB/T 27930 — Communication protocols between off-board conductive charger and battery management system for electric vehicle
GB/T 20234 series — Connection set for conductive charging of electric vehicles
rows.gap: Confirm that the charger model is configured for IEC 61851 charging modes as required by EVCS Directive 1034/2024. Obtain IEC 61851 compliance evidence (test report or certificate) from an accredited body. Verify voltage and frequency settings match Ethiopia 220 V / 50 Hz single-phase or 380 V / 50 Hz three-phase as applicable. If the product was tested only to GB/T 18487, assess the scope differences and arrange supplementary IEC 61851 testing before deployment.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory under EVCS Directive No. 1034/2024 for EV chargers deployed in Ethiopia; EV importers and assemblers must establish compliant charging infrastructure as a market-entry condition
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 18487 is mandatory in China; IEC 61851 compliance is required for Ethiopia market under Directive 1034/2024

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] EVCS Directive No. 1034/2024 mandates IEC 61851 charging modes for Ethiopia. Chinese chargers certified only to GB/T 18487 require technical review and may need supplementary IEC 61851 testing before deployment. Configure for 220 V / 380 V, 50 Hz.

source.publisher: Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia Ministry of Justice — EVCS Directive No. 1034/2024

rows.requirement: Connector Interoperability Gap: GB/T 20234 vs IEC 62196 in the Ethiopian EV Market
rows.target_requirement: Ethiopia's vehicle fleet is diversifying rapidly following the January 2024 ban on ICE vehicle imports and growing EV adoption. EVs entering Ethiopia from multiple origins carry different connector standards: European-standard EVs use IEC 62196 Type 2 (AC) and CCS2 (DC); Chinese-standard EVs use GB/T 20234.2 (AC) and GB/T 20234.3 (DC). The GB/T 20234 and IEC 62196 connectors are physically and electrically incompatible without an adapter. A Chinese charger with only GB/T 20234 sockets will not charge IEC 62196-type vehicles, and vice versa. Exporters of Chinese EV charging infrastructure to Ethiopia must assess the intended vehicle fleet composition and provide multi-standard connectors or confirm connector compatibility with the deployment context.
target_standards: IEC 62196-2 — Plugs, socket-outlets, vehicle connectors and vehicle inlets — Conductive charging of electric vehicles — Part 2: Dimensional compatibility and interchangeability requirements for AC pin and contact-tube accessories
GB/T 20234.2 — Connection set for conductive charging of electric vehicles — Part 2: AC charging coupler
GB/T 20234.3 — Connection set for conductive charging of electric vehicles — Part 3: DC charging coupler
EVCS Directive No. 1034/2024 (connector type requirements — verify current text with Ethiopian Petroleum and Energy Authority)
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese EV chargers manufactured for the domestic market use GB/T 20234.2 (AC Type 2 equivalent with a different pin layout) and GB/T 20234.3 (DC, different from CCS2). These connectors are incompatible with IEC 62196 Type 2 and CCS2 without an adapter. Chinese manufacturers who produce multi-standard chargers with both GB/T 20234 and IEC 62196 / CCS2 outlets can address the Ethiopia interoperability requirement.
rows.gap: Assess the Ethiopia deployment site's vehicle fleet. If non-Chinese EVs (IEC 62196 / CCS2) are present or expected, the charger must provide compatible outlet types. Specify chargers with IEC 62196 Type 2 and CCS2 outlets, or dual-standard units. Confirm the connector type requirements of EVCS Directive 1034/2024 with the Petroleum and Energy Authority before finalising the equipment specification.

mandatory_status.target: Connector type requirements governed by EVCS Directive No. 1034/2024; specific mandatory connector types should be confirmed with the Ethiopian Petroleum and Energy Authority
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 20234 is mandatory in China for domestic charging; IEC 62196 compatibility is a market and regulatory requirement for the Ethiopia deployment context

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] The GB/T 20234 and IEC 62196 connector families are physically incompatible. Ethiopian EV market diversification means GB/T-only chargers will not serve all vehicles. Specify multi-standard or IEC 62196-compatible chargers, and confirm connector requirements with the Petroleum and Energy Authority under Directive 1034/2024.

source.publisher: EV24.africa — Ethiopia's EV Import Regulations: Key Changes

rows.requirement: Ethiopia EV Import Policy and Market Context for Charger Exporters
rows.target_requirement: Ethiopia became the first country to ban ICE vehicle imports effective January 2024, creating a structurally EV-only new vehicle import market. As of May 2025, the ban was extended to SKD and CKD ICE vehicle kits. Fully assembled EVs face a 15% import duty; SKD EV kits face 5%; locally assembled EV parts face near-zero tariffs. Importers and assemblers of EVs must establish EV charging stations as a condition of market entry under EVCS Directive No. 1034/2024. This policy creates a mandatory supply-side requirement for EV charger infrastructure. Charger exporters from China targeting Ethiopia should note: (1) the fast-growing captive demand from mandatory EV-importer charging obligations; (2) the connector and standard compatibility requirements; and (3) that international battery safety certifications are required for imported charger-battery combinations.
target_standards: EVCS Directive No. 1034/2024 (mandatory charging infrastructure as market-entry condition for EV importers)
Ethiopia ICE vehicle import ban (effective January 2024, extended to SKD/CKD May 2025)
Ethiopia EV import duty schedule: 15% fully assembled EVs, 5% SKD EV kits
rows.china_equivalent: China does not have an equivalent ICE import ban. Chinese EV charger manufacturers face different domestic market drivers. For export to Ethiopia, the mandatory charging infrastructure policy under Directive 1034/2024 creates structurally guaranteed demand — but only for chargers that meet IEC 61851 mode requirements and connector compatibility with the deployed Ethiopian EV fleet.
china_standards: GB/T 18487 (Chinese domestic EV charger standard — baseline reference only for Ethiopia market)
rows.gap: Charger exporters targeting the Ethiopian mandatory EV infrastructure market should: confirm IEC 61851 compliance; confirm connector compatibility with the Ethiopian EV fleet mix; verify EVCS Directive 1034/2024 requirements with the Petroleum and Energy Authority; confirm whether ECAE PVoC covers their charger product category; and ensure grid settings match 220 V / 50 Hz Ethiopia specifications.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory: EV importers and assemblers must establish charging stations under Directive 1034/2024 as a market-entry condition; creates structural demand for compliant charger infrastructure
mandatory_status.cn: No equivalent ICE ban or mandatory charging infrastructure obligation in China; Ethiopia policy is market-specific

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Ethiopia's ICE import ban and Directive 1034/2024 create mandatory demand for EV charging infrastructure. Chinese charger exporters should verify IEC 61851 compliance, connector interoperability with the Ethiopian fleet, and ECAE PVoC scope before entering this market.

source.publisher: Further Africa — Ethiopia Drives Towards Electric Mobility: EV Charging Stations Now Mandatory
